results.resolved_title: Salmonella SPI-2 Type III Secretion System Effectors: Molecular Mechanisms And Physiological Consequences.
results.note: Review of SPI-2 T3SS effectors; abstract confirms topic but names no individual protein. Full text paywalled (Elsevier 302). SseA not in retrievable text.

results.resolved_title: Identification of VceA and VceC, two members of the VjbR regulon that are translocated into macrophages by the Type IV secretion system.
results.quote: identification of the proteins encoded by BAB1_1652 (VceA) and BR1038/BAB1_1058 (VceC) as novel protein substrates of theT4SS.
results.note: Brucella VirB T4SS. Full text retrieved; locus BAB1_1058 matches. Spacing artifact 'theT4SS' from italics stripping.

results.resolved_title: Identification of VceA and VceC, two members of the VjbR regulon that are translocated into macrophages by the Type IV secretion system.
results.quote: identification of the proteins encoded by BAB1_1652 (VceA) and BR1038/BAB1_1058 (VceC) as novel protein substrates of theT4SS.
results.note: Brucella VirB T4SS. Locus BAB1_1652 matches VceA. Full text retrieved.

results.resolved_title: Multivalent binding of nonnative substrate proteins by the chaperonin GroEL.
results.quote: The chaperonin GroEL binds nonnative substrate protein in the central cavity of an open ring through exposed hydrophobic residues at the inside aspect of the apical domains
results.note: DOI resolves (PMID 10721993) to a GroEL chaperonin protein-folding study (Farr et al, Cell 2000). No mention of SptP or Salmonella T3SS. Citation mismatch.

results.resolved_title: Shigella deliver an effector protein to trigger host microtubule destabilization, which promotes Rac1 activity and efficient bacterial internalization (VirA).
results.quote: VirA is a novel type of bacterial effector capable of inducing membrane ruffling through the stimulation of MT destabilization.
results.note: Shigella T3SS effector VirA; abstract retrieved via PMC. Title truncated in source feed; reconstructed from abstract topic.

results.resolved_title: Translocation of Helicobacter pylori CagA into gastric epithelial cells by type IV secretion.
results.quote: such strains translocate the bacterial protein CagA into gastric epithelial cells by a type IV secretion system, encoded by the cag pathogenicity island.
results.note: H. pylori CagA via cag-PAI T4SS; abstract retrieved (PMID 10688800). Matches organism and system.

results.resolved_title: Unusual Genetic Organization of a Functional Type I Protein Secretion System in Neisseria meningitidis.
results.quote: Mutation at either locus resulted in an inability to secrete FrpC and a related protein, here called FrpC2.
results.note: Neisseria meningitidis RTX exoprotein FrpC secreted by T1SS (TOSS); abstract retrieved via PMC. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: Type II Secretion-Dependent Degradative and Cytotoxic Activities Mediated by Serine Proteases StmPr1 and StmPr2.
results.quote: the serine proteases StmPr1 and StmPr2, which were confirmed to be Xps substrates, are predominantly responsible for secreted proteolytic activities exhibited by strain K279a
results.note: Stenotrophomonas maltophilia K279a; Xps is the T2SS. StmPr1 confirmed T2SS substrate. Abstract retrieved via PMC.

results.resolved_title: AAA+ proteins: converging mechanisms, diverging functions.
results.quote: AAA+ proteins: converging mechanisms, diverging functions.
results.note: DOI resolves (PMID 32461632) to a conference-proceedings review on AAA+ ATPases (Glynn et al, Nat Struct Mol Biol 2020). No mention of MvcA or Legionella T4SS. Citation mismatch.

results.resolved_title: Eukaryotic-Like Type IV Substrates Interfere with Organelle Trafficking.
results.quote: We found that LegC3-GFP and LegC7/YlfA-GFP caused the mis-secretion of CPY-Invertase
results.note: Full text retrieved. Paper characterizes Legionella Icm/Dot effectors LegC1-C8 (lpg2862 = Lgt2 not mentioned; 'lgt'/'2862'/glucosyltransferase absent). Right organism+system, wrong protein.

results.resolved_title: Bacterial esterases reverse lipopolysaccharide ubiquitylation to block host immunity.
results.quote: following N-terminally GFP-tagged effectors; BapA, BapC, BopC, BopE, BprD, CHBP or VgrG5
results.note: Full text retrieved. BopC listed among 'eight previously defined and verified secreted effectors' in B. thailandensis E264. Paper does NOT specify BopC's secretion system; BopC is canonically a Bsa T3SS effector, not T6SS. Secreted-effector role supported; T6SS tie unconfirmed in-text.

results.resolved_title: The Type VI Secretion System Engages a Redox-Regulated Dual-Functional Heme Transporter for Zinc Acquisition.
results.quote: Burkholderia thailandensis T6SS4 is involved in zinc acquisition via secretion of a zinc-scavenging protein, TseZ, that interacts with the outer membrane heme transporter HmuR.
results.note: B. thailandensis T6SS4 secretes TseZ; abstract retrieved (PMID 28746878). Organism and T6SS match.

results.resolved_title: Structure and sequence analysis of YadA and UspAs reveal a novel class of adhesins.
results.quote: an N-terminal oval head domain is followed by a putative coiled-coil rod and terminated by a C-terminal membrane anchor domain.
results.note: Yersinia YadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (T5cSS). Abstract retrieved via PMC; describes tripartite autotransporter architecture (C-terminal membrane-anchor/translocator) consistent with T5SS. Abstract does not use explicit 'secretion' wording.
